key_risks: Employment report surprise on July 2 could trigger broad equity volatility before close
Geopolitical escalation from Iran strike may increase risk-off flows
Reversal in recent healthcare and biotech gains if momentum fades
Tech sector stabilization could reduce relative outperformance of defensive holdings

portfolio.rationale: Healthcare led market gains on June 26 with EMA approvals providing near-term catalyst support before exit close.
Biotech posted the strongest 7-day return in the data set amid favorable regulatory news flow.
Low-volatility equities delivered positive recent performance with defensive characteristics ahead of employment data.
Small-value equities showed recent outperformance versus broad market with domestic growth sensitivity.

portfolio_rationale: Overweight healthcare and biotech for recent strength and catalysts while adding low-vol and small-value for defensive tilt and relative performance into the July 2 employment report.
rationale_summary: Focus on sectors with positive June 26 momentum and lower sensitivity to tech/AI weakness and geopolitical oil moves over the one-week window.
